skill.name: web-performance-seo
skill.description: Fix PageSpeed Insights/Lighthouse accessibility "!" errors caused by contrast audit failures (CSS filters, OKLCH/OKLAB, low opacity, gradient text, image backgrounds). Use for accessibility-driven SEO/performance debugging and remediation.
skill.summary: Fix PageSpeed Insights and Lighthouse accessibility errors related to color contrast for better SEO performance.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected external_commands patterns and weak cryptographic algorithms. Evaluation reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' are CLI command examples in markdown documentation (ripgrep commands for code scanning), not executable code. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are OKLCH/OKLAB CSS color space specifications misidentified as crypto algorithms. This skill provides documentation and code patterns for fixing accessibility contrast issues - no security risks present.

medium_findings.title: Documentation CLI Commands Detected
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'external_commands' patterns in markdown code blocks. These are CLI command examples (ripgrep) shown as documentation for users to run on their own code, not executable code within the skill itself.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected patterns are markdown code blocks showing CLI examples like 'rg -n backdrop-blur .' These are documentation for users to scan their own code, not code that executes. Backticks are markdown formatting, not Ruby/shell execution operators.

low_findings.title: OKLCH/OKLAB Color Space Misidentified as Crypto
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 23. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - OKLCH/OKLAB are CSS color space specifications (CSS Color Level 4), not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: OKLCH and OKLAB are CSS color functions for the Oklab color space (perceptually uniform). The scanner incorrectly pattern-matched 'oklch/oklab' as weak cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic functionality exists in this skill.

content.value_statement: This skill fixes PageSpeed Insights and Lighthouse accessibility errors caused by color contrast failures. It scans for CSS patterns that trigger accessibility audit failures and provides proven fix patterns to improve your SEO performance.
actual_capabilities: Identify CSS patterns that cause Lighthouse/PageSpeed accessibility failures
Scan codebases for backdrop-blur, mix-blend-mode, and OKLCH/OKLAB color usage
Convert problematic CSS color spaces to accessible alternatives
Fix gradient text with proper color fallbacks
Add proper overlays for text-over-image scenarios
limitations: Does not run Lighthouse or PageSpeed tests - only provides guidance
Cannot automatically modify files - provides patterns for manual application
Does not test the fixes locally - recommends using Lighthouse/axe for verification
Does not cover all accessibility issues - focuses specifically on color contrast

use_cases.title: Fix Failed Accessibility Audits
use_cases.description: When Lighthouse shows '!' instead of a score in the accessibility section, use this skill to identify and fix the CSS patterns causing the failure.

use_cases.title: Improve SEO Through Accessibility
use_cases.description: Accessibility is a ranking factor. Use this skill to fix contrast issues that negatively impact search engine rankings.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Code Review
use_cases.description: Review CSS and components for accessibility issues before deployment to catch contrast problems early.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Contrast Fix Request
prompt_templates.prompt: My Lighthouse accessibility audit shows '!' instead of a score. The color-contrast audit is failing. Help me identify and fix the CSS patterns causing this issue.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a failing accessibility audit in Lighthouse

prompt_templates.title: OKLCH Color Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: I am using OKLCH colors in my CSS and they are causing accessibility issues in Lighthouse. Show me how to convert OKLCH to accessible HSL or RGB alternatives.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has OKLCH/OKLAB colors causing contrast failures

prompt_templates.title: Gradient Text Accessibility
prompt_templates.prompt: My website uses gradient text but Lighthouse flags color-contrast issues. Provide a pattern for gradient text with proper color fallbacks that pass accessibility audits.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has gradient text causing contrast failures

prompt_templates.title: Text Overlay on Images
prompt_templates.prompt: I have text over images and the contrast is failing accessibility audits. Show me the correct pattern for adding overlays to ensure sufficient contrast ratios.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has text over images with poor contrast

output_examples.input: My website shows '!' in Lighthouse accessibility instead of a score
output_examples.output: The '!' indicates an audit failure. Common causes include: CSS backdrop-blur or filters, OKLCH/OKLAB colors, low opacity backgrounds (< 0.4), gradient text with transparent color, or text over images without overlays. I recommend scanning your codebase with: `rg -n "backdrop-blur|filter:|mix-blend-mode" .` and similar commands to identify problematic patterns.

output_examples.input: How do I fix the OKLCH colors in my CSS for accessibility?
output_examples.output: Convert OKLCH to HSL or RGB. For example, `--primary: oklch(0.55 0.22 264);` becomes `--primary: hsl(250, 60%, 50%);`. Use a color picker or tool to match the visual appearance while using a standard color space that Lighthouse can analyze correctly.

best_practices: Always test locally with Lighthouse or axe before deploying
Raise background opacity to 0.4 or higher (prefer 0.6+)
Use forced-colors media query for Windows high contrast mode support
Verify contrast ratios meet 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text
anti_patterns: Using CSS filters or backdrop-blur without checking accessibility impact
Using OKLCH/OKLAB colors without testing in Lighthouse
Setting text color to transparent over gradient backgrounds
Placing text directly over images without proper overlay

faq.question: What does the '!' mean in Lighthouse accessibility?
faq.answer: The '!' indicates the accessibility audit failed completely and could not compute a score. This typically happens when Lighthouse cannot analyze certain CSS patterns like filters or color spaces.

faq.question: Can this skill run Lighthouse tests for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and code patterns. You need to run Lighthouse or PageSpeed Insights yourself to verify the fixes.

faq.question: Why are OKLCH colors causing issues?
faq.answer: Lighthouse uses getImageData to analyze colors, which has limited support for newer CSS color spaces like OKLCH and OKLAB. Converting to HSL or RGB ensures accurate analysis.

faq.question: What opacity level is safe for accessibility?
faq.answer: Use background opacity of 0.4 or higher. Prefer 0.6 or higher for better contrast. Tailwind classes like /10 and /20 are too low; use /40, /60, or higher.

faq.question: Does fixing accessibility actually help SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. Accessibility is a Google ranking factor. Sites with better accessibility scores may perform better in search results.

faq.question: How do I test fixes locally before deploying?
faq.answer: Use Lighthouse in Chrome DevTools or the axe browser extension. Run tests locally to verify fixes before deploying to production.
